name: pragma-sdk

metadata.name: pragma-sdk
metadata.summary: Core package for rollup-native Pragma Oracle

documentation:
# Pragma SDK

Main repository containing our SDK code.

For more information, please check the documentation:

https://pragma-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html

functions.name: pragma_sdk.offchain.signer.build_publish_message
documentation:
Builds a typed data message to publish spot entries on the Pragma API.
see <https://community.starknet.io/t/snip-off-chain-signatures-a-la-eip712> for reference

```
param entries

List of SpotEntry objects
```

classes.name: pragma_sdk.offchain.signer.OffchainSigner

methods.name: pragma_sdk.offchain.signer.OffchainSigner.sign_publish_message
documentation:
Sign a publish message

```
param entries

List of SpotEntry objects

return

Tuple containing the signature and the message hash
```
Class used to sign messages for the Pragma API

functions.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.types.contract._multicall
documentation:
Allows for a callback in the invocation of a contract method.
This is useful for tracking the nonce changes

functions.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.types.contract._invoke
documentation:
Allows for a callback in the invocation of a contract method.
This is useful for tracking the nonce changes

functions.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.utils.pick_random_rpc
documentation:
Picks a random RPC endpoint from the list and verifies it's working.
Tests each RPC with a spec version request until finding a working one.

functions.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.utils.get_rpc_url
documentation:
Returns the RPC URL for the given network.
Will return a random URL in the list if the network is "sepolia" or "mainnet".

```
param network

Network to get the RPC URL for.

param port

Port to use for the RPC URL.

return

RPC URL.
```

functions.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.utils.get_full_node_client_from_network
documentation: Create a new full node client for the passed network/port (rpc url).

classes.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.client.PragmaOnChainClient

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.client.PragmaOnChainClient._setup_contracts
documentation:
Setup the contracts for the client.
For now, this includes the Oracle and PublisherRegistry contracts.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.client.PragmaOnChainClient._process_secret_key
documentation: Converts a Private Key to a KeyPair.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.client.PragmaOnChainClient.account_address
documentation: Return the account address.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.client.PragmaOnChainClient.get_balance
documentation:
Get the balance of an account given the account contract address and token address.

```
param account_contract_address

The account contract address.

param token_address

The token address. If None, will use ETH as the token address.

return

The balance of the account.
```

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.client.PragmaOnChainClient.get_block_number
documentation: Returns the current block number.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.client.PragmaOnChainClient.publish_entries
documentation:
Publish entries on-chain.

```
param entries

List of Entry objects

return

List of InvokeResult objects
```

Client for interacting with Pragma on Starknet.

```
param network

Target network for the client. Can be a URL string, or one of
"mainnet", "sepolia" or "devnet"

param account_private_key

Optional private key for requests. Not necessary if not making
network updates.
Can be either an hexadecimal string 0x prefixed, an integer or
a KeyStore type.
The KeyStore is a tuple of two string [str, str], which are
["path/to/the/keystore", "password_to_unlock_the_keystore"].

param account_contract_address

Optional account contract address.  Not necessary if not
making network updates.
Can either be an integer or an hexadecimal string 0x prefixed.

param contract_addresses_config

Optional Contract Addresses for Pragma contracts.
Will default to the provided network but must be set if using
non standard contracts.

param port

Optional port to interact with local node. Will default to 5050.

param chain_name

A str-representation of the chain if a URL string is given for .
Must be one of "mainnet", "sepolia" or "devnet".
```

functions.name: pragma_sdk.offchain.client.get_endpoint_publish_offchain
documentation: Returns the correct publish endpoint for the given data type.

classes.name: pragma_sdk.offchain.client.PragmaAPIClient

methods.name: pragma_sdk.offchain.client.PragmaAPIClient.get_ohlc
documentation:
Retrieve OHLC data from the Pragma API.

```
param pair

Pair to get data for

param timestamp

Timestamp to get data for, defaults to now

param interval

Interval on which data is aggregated, defaults to 1m

param aggregation

Aggregation method, defaults to Median

return

[EntryResult] result data
```

methods.name: pragma_sdk.offchain.client.PragmaAPIClient.publish_entries
documentation:
Publishes spot and future entries to the Pragma API.
This function accepts both type of entries - but they need to be sent through
different endpoints & signed differently, so we split them in two separate
lists.

```
param entries

List of SpotEntry objects
```

methods.name: pragma_sdk.offchain.client.PragmaAPIClient._create_entries
documentation:
Publishes entries to the Pragma API & returns the http response.

We can only publish entries of the same type at once - if we have a mix of
types, the function will raise an error.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.offchain.client.PragmaAPIClient.get_entry
documentation:
Get data aggregated on the Pragma API.

```
param pair

Pair to get data for

param timestamp

Timestamp to get data for, defaults to now

param interval

Interval on which data is aggregated, defaults to 2h

param routing

If we want to route data for unexisting pair, defaults to False

param aggregation

Aggregation method, defaults to TWAP

return

[EntryResult] result data
```

methods.name: pragma_sdk.offchain.client.PragmaAPIClient.get_future_entry
documentation:
Get data aggregated on the Pragma API.

```
param pair

Pair to get data for

param timestamp

Timestamp to get data for, defaults to now

param interval

Interval on which data is aggregated, defaults to 2h

param routing

If we want to route data for unexisting pair, defaults to False

param aggregation

Aggregation method, defaults to TWAP

return

[EntryResult] result data
```

```
Start timestamp
```

documentation:
Get expiries list for a future pair on the Pragma API.


Client for interacting with the Pragma API.
see https://docs.pragma.build/Resources/PragmApi/overview

An API Key is required to interact with the API.

classes.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.merkle_feed.MerkleFeedMixin

documentation: Returns the latest OptionData for the given instrument name.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.merkle_feed.MerkleFeedMixin.update_options_data
documentation: Update the Option data upon merkle proof verification.

Class used to retrieve values from the Deribit options Merkle Feed.
The Merkle Feed is in fact a merkle root stored in a GenericEntry.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.summary.SummaryStatsMixin.calculate_mean
documentation:
Calculates the mean for a given data feeds.

```
param asset

Asset for which we want to compute the mean

param start

Start timestamp

param stop

End timestamp

param aggregation_mode

AggregationMode

return

Tuple[int, int] (mean, decimals)
```

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.summary.SummaryStatsMixin.calculate_volatility
documentation:
Calculates the volatility for a given data feeds.

```
param asset

Asset for which we want to compute the volatility

param start_tick

Starting timestamp

param end_tick

End timestamp

param num_samples

Number of samples used to compute the volatility

param aggregation_mode

AggregationMode

return

Tuple[int, int] (volatility, decimals)
```

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.summary.SummaryStatsMixin.calculate_twap
documentation:
Calculates the TWAP for a given data feeds.
see <https://docs.pragma.build/Resources/Cairo%201/computational-feeds/TWAP> for more info
The TWAP will be calculated between start_time and start_time+time.

```
param asset

Asset for which we want to compute the volatility

param aggregation_mode

AggregationMode

param time

The duration (in seconds ) over which you want to calculate the TWAP.

param start_time

The start time (in seconds) from which you want to calculate the TWAP.

return

Tuple[int, int] (twap, decimals)
```

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.nonce.NonceMixin._get_nonce
documentation:
Returns the nonce that should be used for the next transaction.
Steps:
1. Update nonces' statuses.
2. Cleanup nonce_dict.
3. Return the pending nonce if there is one.
4. Return the latest nonce if there are no pending nonces.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.nonce.NonceMixin.cleanup_nonce_dict
documentation: Cleanup the nonce_dict by removing all txs that have been either rejected or accepted.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.nonce.NonceMixin.track_nonce
documentation:
Callback function to track the nonce of a transaction.
Will update the nonce_dict and pending_nonce attributes.
We track the nonce used for a given transaction hash.

```
param nonce

The nonce of the transaction.

param transaction_hash

The hash of the transaction.
```

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.nonce.NonceMixin.update_nonce_dict
documentation: Update the statuses of the nonces in the nonce_dict.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.nonce.NonceMixin.get_nonce
documentation:
Get the nonce of the account contract address.
Just a wrapper around the client's get_contract_nonce method.

```
param include_pending

Whether to include pending transactions in the nonce calculation.

param block_number

Custom block number to get the nonce from.
```

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.nonce.NonceMixin.get_status
documentation:
Tries to get the status of a transaction by its hash.

```
param transaction_hash

The hash of the transaction.

param check_interval

The interval in seconds between each check.

param retries

The number of retries before giving up.

return

The status of the transaction.
```

Taken from:

Request randomness from the VRF contract.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.randomness.RandomnessMixin._get_submit_or_refund_calls
documentation:
Returns a Sequence of Call necesary to either submit or refund the provided
request.

If the caller is whitelisted, we don't check if there's enough fees for the call
to work. We will pay for the fees if there isn't enough.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.randomness.RandomnessMixin.submit_random_multicall
documentation:
Submit randomness to the VRF contract using a multicall.

If fee estimation fails, the status of the request is updated to OUT_OF_GAS.
Then, the remaining gas is refunded to the requestor address.

For callers that are in the whitelisted_addresses set, we won't check if they
have enough ETH for the callback fees.

Fee estimation is used to set the callback fee parameter in the VRFSubmitParams object.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.randomness.RandomnessMixin.estimate_gas_submit_random_op
documentation:
Estimate the gas for the submit_random operation.

```
param vrf_submit_params

param execution_config

ExecutionConfig object containing the execution parameters.

return

The estimated gas for the operation.
```

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.randomness.RandomnessMixin._index_randomness_requests_events
documentation:
Indexes all the Randomness request events from the [from_block] block
to [to_block] and returns them.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.randomness.RandomnessMixin.handle_random
documentation:
Handle randomness requests.
Will submit randomness for requests that are not too old and have not been handled yet.

The steps are:
    1. Index the RandomnessRequest events if they're not provided in the [requests_events]
       parameter,
    2. Fetch the status of the requests & only keep the RECEIVED requests,
    3. Fetch the block hash of each events,
    4. Compute the VRF submissions of all events,
    5. Submit all the submissions through a multicall in [submit_random_multicall].

methods.name: pragma_sdk.onchain.mixins.randomness.RandomnessMixin.fetch_block_hashes
documentation:
Fetch the block_hash of all events in parallel.
--
TODO: Really not optimal but not a bottleneck at the moment.
We should be fetching only pending + latest and attribute that to
the events, instead of doing N times (n = len of events).

```
Dict between the quote currency and the new quote currency
```

documentation:
For every unique base assets in the provided pairs list, fetch all the

Retrieves all the options data for all available instruments for a set of pairs.

All the available prices are put in a Merkle tree which is sent in a GenericEntry.

Class responsible of storing LP data using Redis Lists for chronological order.

The current layout is:
    ├── mainnet
    │   └── 0x068c...
    │       ├── reserves: [reserves_0, reserves_1, ..., reserves_N]
    │       └── total_supply: [total_supply_0, total_supply_1, ..., total_supply_N]
    └── sepolia
         └── (...)

classes.name: pragma_sdk.common.types.entry.GenericEntry

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.types.entry.GenericEntry.from_oracle_response
documentation:
Builds the object from a PragmaAsset and an OracleResponse.
Method primarly used by our price pusher package when we're retrieving
lastest oracle prices for comparisons with the latest prices of
various APIs (binance etc).

Represents a Generic Entry.

Currently used this way:
instead of publishing all the future options for all availables instruments from Deribit,
we place them in all a Merkle tree & we only publish the merkle root through this Generic entry.
So the key will be DERIBIT_OPTIONS_MERKLE_ROOT and the value the merkle root containing
all the price feeds.

variables.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetchers.ekubo.PERIOD
documentation: one hour

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetchers.ekubo.EkuboFetcher.fetch
documentation: Fetches the data from the fetcher and returns a list of Entry objects.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetchers.ekubo.EkuboFetcher._get_no_quote_errors
documentation: Returns errors for the pairs that have a Quote currency without address.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetchers.ekubo.EkuboFetcher._call_get_prices
documentation:
Calls the get_prices function from the Price Fetcher contract and returns
the response.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetchers.ekubo.EkuboFetcher._parse_response_into_entries
documentation:
Parse response data into a list of entries or errors.
First element is the number of responses.
Following elements are either:
    - Status (0: NotInitialized, 1: InsufficientLiquidity, 2: PeriodTooLong, 3: Price)
    - If status is 3 (PRICE_AVAILABLE), next two elements are price (low, high)

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetchers.ekubo.EkuboFetcher._handle_price_status
documentation:
Handle the sub-array of the Ekubo Response when the response was 3, i.e
PRICE_AVAILABLE.

The steps are:
    * 1. Convert the UINT256 price from Ekubo to int,
    * 2. Convert this cairo price to a correct price for the pair,
    * 3. If the pair has been hopped, convert it back to the original pair,
    * 4. Create the new Entry.

At the end, we return the created Entry and the number of elements used
in the Cairo response.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetchers.ekubo.EkuboFetcher._adapt_back_hopped_pair
documentation:
If a hop occured for the given pair, we enter this function.

For example, if we just fetched the price of the pair BTC/USDC
but we know that a hop occured with USD, we convert it back
to BTC/USD.

To do so, we use the hop_prices of USDC/USD and compute
the conversion between the two pairs.

At the end, we return the original Pair before hop and the price.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetchers.ekubo.EkuboFetcher._handle_error_status
documentation:
Handle error statuses (0, 1, 2) - i.e, return the correct PublisherFetchError
with the right message.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetchers.ekubo.EkuboFetcher._convert_raw_cairo_price
documentation:
Converts a Raw price returned from the Ekubo Price Fetcher contract to
a price with the right decimals depending on the pair.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetchers.ekubo.EkuboFetcher._get_pairs_after_hop
documentation:
Returns the Fetcher pairs after the work of the Hop Handler.
Each pair is associated with a boolean flag allowing us to know if
the pair has been hopped.

This flag is used later when grouping currencies, so we can attach this
boolean to each hopped quote currencies and after reconstruct the original pair.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetchers.ekubo.EkuboFetcher._group_pairs_by_quote
documentation:
Groups a list of Pair by their quote currency.

Example, with this set of pairs:
    - Pair(BTC, USD)
    - Pair(ETH, USD)
    - Pair(DOGE, ETH)

We will get:
{
    (USD, false): [BTC, ETH],
    (ETH, false): [DOGE]
}

all elements being of type `Currency`.

The boolean in the key will be True if the quote currency was hopped,
else false.

classes.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetcher_client.FetcherClient

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetcher_client.FetcherClient.add_fetchers
documentation: Add fetchers to the supported fetchers list.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetcher_client.FetcherClient.add_fetcher
documentation: Add a single fetcher to the supported fetchers list.

methods.name: pragma_sdk.common.fetchers.fetcher_client.FetcherClient.fetch
documentation:
Fetch data from all fetchers asynchronously.
Fetching is done in parallel for all fetchers.

```
param filter_exceptions

If True, filters out exceptions from the result list

param return_exceptions

If True, returns exceptions in the result list

param timeout_duration

Timeout duration for each fetcher

return

List of fetched data
```

This client extends the pragma client with functionality for fetching from our third party sources.
It can be used to synchronously or asynchronously fetch assets.

The client works by setting up fetchers that are provided the assets to fetch and the publisher name.

Example usage:

```python
pairs = [
    Pair.from_tickers("BTC", "USD"),
    Pair.from_tickers("ETH", "USD"),
]

bitstamp_fetcher = BitstampFetcher(pairs, "publisher_test")
gateio_fetcher = GateIOFetcher(pairs, "publisher_test")

fetchers = [
    bitstamp_fetcher,
    gateio_fetcher,
]

fc = FetcherClient()
fc.add_fetchers(fetchers)

await fc.fetch()
fc.fetch_sync()
```

You can also set a custom timeout duration as followed:

```python
await fc.fetch(timeout_duration=20)  # Denominated in seconds (default=10)
```

modules.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils
documentation: Copyright (C) 2020 Eric Schorn, NCC Group Plc; Provided under the MIT license
This code follows the (IETF) IRTF CFRG Verifiable Random Functions (VRFs) spec *very* closely.
Please refer to https://tools.ietf.org/pdf/draft-irtf-cfrg-vrf-06.pdf
https://github.com/nccgroup/draft-irtf-cfrg-vrf-06

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils.ecvrf_prove
documentation:
Input:
    secret_key - VRF private key (32 bytes)
    alpha_string - input alpha, an octet string
Output:
    ("VALID", pi_string) - where pi_string is the VRF proof, octet string of length ptLen+n+qLen
    (80) bytes, or ("INVALID", []) upon failure
Public API
Section 5.1. ECVRF Proving

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils.ecvrf_proof_to_hash
documentation:
Input:
    pi_string - VRF proof, octet string of length ptLen+n+qLen (80) bytes
Output:
    ("VALID", beta_string) where beta_string is the VRF hash output, octet string
    of length hLen (64) bytes, or ("INVALID", []) upon failure
Section 5.2. ECVRF Proof To Hash

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils.ecvrf_verify
documentation:
Input:
    y - public key, an EC point as bytes
    pi_string - VRF proof, octet string of length ptLen+n+qLen (80) bytes
    alpha_string - VRF input, octet string
Output:
    ("VALID", beta_string), where beta_string is the VRF hash output, octet string
    of length hLen (64) bytes; or ("INVALID", []) upon failure
Section 5.3. ECVRF Verifying

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils.get_public_key
documentation: Calculate and return the public_key as an encoded point string (bytes)

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._ecvrf_hash_to_curve_elligator2_25519
documentation:
Input:
    suite_string - a single octet specifying ECVRF ciphersuite.
    alpha_string - value to be hashed, an octet string
    y - public key, an EC point as bytes
Output:
    H - hashed value, a finite EC point in G, or INVALID upon failure
Fixed options:
    p = 2^255-19, the size of the finite field F, a prime, for edwards25519 and curve25519 curves
    A = 486662, Montgomery curve constant for curve25519
    cofactor = 8, the cofactor for edwards25519 and curve25519 curves
Internal functions
Section 5.4.1.2. ECVRF_hash_to_curve_elligator2_25519

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._ecvrf_nonce_generation_rfc8032
documentation:
Input:
    secret_key - an ECVRF secret key as bytes
    h_string - an octet string
Output:
    k - an integer between 0 and q-1
5.4.2.2. ECVRF Nonce Generation From RFC 8032

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._ecvrf_hash_points
documentation:
Input:
    P1...PM - EC points in G
Output:
    c - hash value, integer between 0 and 2^(8n)-1
Section 5.4.3. ECVRF Hash Points

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._ecvrf_decode_proof
documentation:
Input:
    pi_string - VRF proof, octet string (ptLen+n+qLen octets)
Output:
    "INVALID", or Gamma - EC point
    c - integer between 0 and 2^(8n)-1
    s - integer between 0 and 2^(8qLen)-1
Section 5.4.4. ECVRF Decode Proof

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._assert_and_sample
documentation:
Input:
    key - key for assert values, basename (+ '_sample') for sampled values.
Output:
    None; asserts actuals then and assigns into global test_dict
If key exists, assert dict expected value against provided actual value.
Sample actual value and store into test_dict under key + '_sample'.

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._edwards_add
documentation: Edwards curve point addition
Much of the following code has been adapted from ed25519.py
at https://ed25519.cr.yp.to/software.html retrieved 27 Dec 2019.
While it is gloriously inefficient, it provides an excellent demonstration of the underlying math.
For example, production code would likely avoid inversion via Fermat's little theorem as it is
extremely expensive with a cost of ~300 field multiplies.

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._encode_point
documentation: Encode point to string containing LSB OF X and 254 bits of y

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._decode_point
documentation: Decode string containing LSB of X and 254 bits of y into point. Checks on-curve. May return "INVALID"

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._get_bit
documentation: Return specified bit from string for subsequent testing

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._get_secret_scalar
documentation: Calculate and return the secret_scalar integer

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._hash
documentation: Return 64-byte SHA512 hash of arbitrary-length byte message

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._short_hash
documentation: Return 64-byte SHA512 hash of arbitrary-length byte message... trimmed to 31 byte felt

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._inverse
documentation: Calculate inverse via Fermat's little theorem

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._is_on_curve
documentation: Check to confirm point is on curve; return boolean

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._scalar_multiply
documentation: Scalar multiplied by curve point

functions.name: pragma_sdk.common.randomness.randomness_utils._x_recover
documentation: Recover x coordinate from y coordinate
